label: Qualitative Research
definition: Research methodology using focus groups and in-depth interviews to explore attitudes, perceptions, and behaviors in depth

label: Focus Group
definition: Moderated group discussion with 8-10 recruited participants exploring specific topics; 6 groups conducted (3 Boston, 3 San Diego)

label: In-Depth Interview
definition: Individual or family direct interviews; 11 conducted among parent/child dyads or triads across Boston and San Diego

label: Product Demonstration
definition: Live demonstration of SD memory card interchangeability across devices shown to focus group participants

label: High Confidence
definition: Finding strongly supported by consistent responses across multiple focus groups and IDIs

label: Medium Confidence
definition: Finding supported by majority of respondents but with some notable variation across groups or markets

label: Low Confidence
definition: Finding based on limited respondent input, minority views, or single-market observations

label: Strategy Classification
definition: Classification of a strategic approach, market positioning, or competitive strategy

label: Viability Prediction
definition: Prediction about the future viability or success of a technology, product, or company

label: Actual Outcome
definition: Verified actual outcome of a prediction, strategy, or market trend

label: Technology Assessment
definition: Assessment of a technology capabilities, limitations, or competitive position

label: Benchmark Result
definition: Quantitative or qualitative benchmark measurement from the study

label: Framework Factor
definition: Factor identified as part of an analytical framework for understanding the market

label: Market Data
definition: Market data point, statistic, or quantitative finding

label: Expert Opinion
definition: Expert opinion, analyst assessment, or professional judgment from the study authors
